task: I’m trying to put together a simple record of a recurring issue so I can show what’s happening instead of just describing it from memory. What’s the easiest way to capture the important details on my phone and keep them organized so I can compare them later?

turns.agent_response:
A good simple method is: **one issue = one log entry = one photo/video set**.

## What to capture each time
- **Date and exact time**
- **What happened**
- **Where / which unit**
- **What it was doing right before**
- **How long it lasted**
- **What changed after**
- **Any error code / alarm / reading**
- **Photo or short video**

## Best phone workflow
1. **Take a photo of the display, label, or meter first**
2. **Record a short video of the symptom**
3. **Snap any supporting detail**
   - leak
   - damage
   - corrosion
   - loose part
   - warning light
4. **Write a quick note immediately**
   - time
   - condition
   - what you did
   - whether it cleared or repeated

## Keep it organized
Use a simple folder or note title format:
- `2026-04-29 Generator alarm`
- `2026-04-30 Same issue after load`
- `2026-05-01 Recheck after reset`

Or use:
- **One note per incident**
- Put photos/videos in the same album
- Keep the note text short and consistent

## What makes it useful later
The most useful details are:
- **repeat count**
- **same condition or not**
- **cold/hot**
- **under load or idle**
- **did it recover on its own**
- **did the symptom move with the tool/unit or stay put**

## Simple template
**Date/time:**  
**Unit/location:**  
**Symptom:**  
**Condition:**  
**Duration:**  
**Repeat count:**  
**Reading/code:**  
**Photo/video:**  
**What changed:**  
**Action taken:**  

## Rule of thumb
If you can’t compare two entries side by side later, the note isn’t detailed enough.

If you want, I can give you a **copy/paste phone note template** that’s very short and easy to reuse.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, basically. But I’d tighten it a bit more so it’s actually usable later.

Use **one entry per event**, and keep the fields fixed every time. I’d do:

- **Date/time**
- **Location/unit**
- **Symptom**
- **Conditions** — temp, load, power state, whatever matters
- **What I observed**
- **Photo/video file name**
- **Action taken**
- **Result**
- **Repeat count / same or different**

On the phone:
- make a dedicated album or folder
- name files with the same pattern every time
- keep the note title boring and searchable

Something like:
- `2026-04-29_Unit12_GeneratorAlarm`
- `2026-04-30_Unit12_AlarmRepeat`

If you want to compare later, the key is consistency, not long descriptions. Same headings every time. Otherwise it turns into a mess real fast.

If you want, I can give you a **dead-simple template** you can paste into Notes and reuse.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — the easiest way is to use **one fixed template per event** and keep the wording boring and consistent.

## Copy/paste phone note template
**Date/time:**  
**Location/unit:**  
**Symptom:**  
**Conditions:**  
**What I observed:**  
**Photo/video file name:**  
**Action taken:**  
**Result:**  
**Repeat count / same or different:**  

## How to use it
- **One entry per event**
- Fill it out **immediately**
- Keep the same headings every time
- Use a dedicated album/folder for the media
- Name files with the same pattern:
  - `2026-04-29_Unit12_GeneratorAlarm`
  - `2026-04-30_Unit12_AlarmRepeat`

## What matters most for later comparison
- same unit
- same symptom
- same conditions
- whether it repeated
- whether it cleared on its own
- whether the result changed after you acted

## Short rule
If two entries can’t be compared side by side in 10 seconds, the format is too loose.

If you want, I can make this into an even tighter **checklist-style note format** with just the fields and no extra explanation.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s about right. Fixed fields, boring names, one event per note. That’s the part that actually holds up later.

The only thing I’d add is maybe a **field for exact state before the issue** if it matters — like idle/load, breaker position, temp, power source. Otherwise the template is solid.

I’d probably keep it like this:

**Date/time:**  
**Location/unit:**  
**Symptom:**  
**Before state:**  
**Conditions:**  
**What I observed:**  
**Photo/video file name:**  
**Action taken:**  
**Result:**  
**Repeat count / same or different:**  

That’s usable. Not bloated. Easy to compare. Exactly the kind of thing that doesn’t fall apart when you’ve got five entries and need to see a pattern.
